programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Eat your vegetables! Martha prepares: spring peas with mint, brown-sugared carrots, sauteed broccoli rabe, roasted acorn squash, corn on the cob, and lemony kale salad

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Martha shares techniques for three homemade stocks: chicken (including a pressure-cooker version), beef, and vegetable. Plus, cooking and storage tips

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.desc: Martha shares her love of houseplants and brings the outdoors into her home. Learn all about the most popular and sought after houseplants, topiaries and begonias and how to take care of them.

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.desc: Succulents are known for their vibrant colors, interesting textures, and often beautiful blooms. Martha explores how best to care for succulents including watering, fertilizing, and repotting.

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.sub-title: Inside Martha's Vegetable Greenhouse; Seed Starting
programme.desc: Martha takes us inside her vegetable greenhouse to see how she has turned her harvest into a year-round bounty. Then, how she collects, stores, and sows seeds. Plus Martha makes her perfect green drink.

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.desc: Martha visits the incredible Orchid Show at the New York Botanical Garden. This year's show immerses you in a mesmerizing array of orchids designed by acclaimed artist Lily Kwong.

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.desc: Meet Martha's chickens! Now with over 200 chickens and 20 different breeds, Martha shares her vast knowledge about raising and caring for her flock. Then learn the how-tos of building the a coop.

programme.title: Martha Gardens
programme.sub-title: Martha Builds a Raised Bed Vegetable Garden
programme.desc: Martha builds a brand new, half-acre raised bed vegetable garden on her farm! It's a step-by-step guide to designing, building, preparing the soil, and planting.

programme.title: Martha Cooks
programme.sub-title: One Pot Wonders With Jamie Oliver
programme.desc: Martha is in the test kitchen with English chef, restaurateur, and author Jamie Oliver, and they're cooking up a mouth-watering menu of dishes that are easy to make.

programme.title: Martha Cooks
programme.desc: Martha and her niece Sophie visit their favorite tea shop, Arogya, in Westport, Conn., to discover the do's and don'ts of making tea; they return to Martha's kitchen to create dishes with tea.

programme.title: Martha Cooks
programme.desc: Martha visits Mast Market in Mt. Kisco, N.Y., and learns the art behind making their renowned chocolate; from roasting cacao, to stone grinding, to forming delicious bars and chips.

programme.title: Martha Cooks
programme.desc: Martha shares techniques for making the perfect burgers; Pat LaFrieda joins Martha to grind his beef mix; Thomas Joseph joins to make a hake fish burger and Martha shares her turkey burger.

programme.title: Martha Cooks

programme.desc: Martha serves up delicious homemade pizzas alongside friends and pizza chefs Christian Petroni and Dean Medico; featuring classic pizzas, signature sauces, and more.

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Butcher Evan Lobel joins Martha for a master class on meat. Learn how to choose and prepare the best cuts of meat to save you money

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Starting with a perfect pot of fluffy white rice, Martha demonstrates her techniques for a flavorful pilaf and a delicious Thai-style fried rice

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Homemade dressings don't compare to store-bought. Martha shows you how easy it is to make your own: vinaigrette, creamy blue cheese dressing, homemade mayonnaise, and aioli

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Steaming is one of the fastest and healthiest ways to cook. Martha shows how to cook in parchment paper, clean and steam mussels, and use a bamboo steamer to prepare salmon and peas

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Roasting is an adaptable technique that works well for meat, fish, chicken, and vegetables. Martha prepares roast chicken, beef tenderloin, and autumn vegetable salad

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: With little hands-on time and no special equipment, braising produces hearty, flavorful dishes. Martha shares recipes for pot roast, braised cabbage, and pulled pork shoulder

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Martha demonstrates healthful and versatile poached chicken breasts. She also shares recipes and techniques for poaching salmon and poaching eggs

programme.title: Martha Stewart's Cooking School
programme.desc: Fried foods should be golden brown and never greasy. Martha deep-fries and pan-fries to perfection: French fries, pan-fried chicken, and Japanese tempura vegetables

programme.desc: Martha teaches us grain substitutions for three classics: rhubarb and raspberry rye crisp, buttermilk barley biscuits, and spelt layer cake with whipped cream and berries

programme.title: Martha Bakes
programme.desc: Martha shows how to make three coffee shop favorites at home: scrumptious apple cider doughnut cake, pumpkin spelt scones with maple glaze, and seeded graham biscotti

programme.title: Martha Bakes
programme.desc: Martha updates three cookie favorites: pecan, oat, and dark chocolate chunk cookies, chewy molasses cookies, and buckwheat-espresso cookies.

programme.title: Martha Bakes
programme.desc: Martha makes pies with healthier crust alternatives, including plum galette with cornmeal crust and dairy-free chocolate-coconut pie. Plus, a mushroom tart made with tahini

programme.title: Martha Bakes
programme.desc: Martha creates three baked goods using unique flours beyond the white variety: herb quiche with rye crust, muesli coffee cake, and seeded savory quick bread

programme.title: Martha Bakes

programme.title: Martha Bakes
programme.desc: Martha makes better-for-you bake sale classics: lemon-yogurt cupcakes with raspberry frosting, whole-wheat sticky buns, orange-barley pound cake, and fluffy whey biscuits

programme.title: Emeril Live

programme.title: Emeril Live
programme.desc: Low carb can be high in flavor! Flank Steak with Mushroom Ragout; Tuna Tacos with Guacamole and Charred Pico de Gallo; Cajun Turkey Sausage with Eggs and Asparagus Sauce; Asian Pork Tenderloin Salad

programme.title: Emeril Live
programme.desc: They're staple recipes on many backroad kitchen tables: Stuffed Eggplant Pirogues; Brisket-Stuffed with Cornbread; Old-Time Corn Soup with Sausage

programme.title: Emeril Live
programme.desc: Ooey Gooey cheese recipes include: Karen's French Harvest Soup; Mashed Potatoes with Cantal; Roasted Red Onions Stuffed with Mascarpone Mousse; Asparagus Tart with Vacherin

programme.title: Emeril Live
programme.desc: Basic Pizza Dough with Parmesan and Oregano Variations; Garlic Knots; Pulled Pork BBQ Pizza; Roasted Garlic White Pizza; Sicilian Double-Stuffed Pizza

programme.title: Emeril Live
programme.desc: It's over-the-top bold flavors with recipes including: Olive-Stuffed Leg of Lamb; Roasted Garlic and Onion Soup; Anchovy Bread; Roasted Figs with Gorgonzola

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Emeril is going back to the basics. Whether you're a novice cook or an advanced chef, you'll love these recipes: vegetable soup, and roast chicken served with roasted root vegetables

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Cozy up with Emeril's warming and comforting classics, including hearty chicken pot pie topped with puff-pastry crust and Emeril's super-tasty spicy meatloaf

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Emeril shows the most useful kitchen equipment: quick and easy stir-fried squid with chili sauce and sirloin steak fajitas

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Emeril shares his burger favorites: buffalo burgers with bacon, cheese, and fresh thyme and Emeril's turkey burgers with cilantro mayonnaise

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Emeril prepares oysters and mussels, including oysters on the half shell with three sauces. Plus, Emeril's classic Moules mariniere with pommes frites

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: It's a chocolate celebration when Emeril makes his ultimate triple chocolate cake: moist chocolate cake layered with chocolate mousse and topped with dark chocolate ganache

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Root for your home team with Emeril's sports party favorites: French dip sandwich, tuna poker chips, and the ultimate cheese dip - cheesy beer!

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Tired of the same old chicken recipes? Emeril's prepares two exciting new chicken recipes: tagine of chicken with preserved lemons and olives and prosciutto- chicken roulade served with chard

programme.title: Emeril's Table
programme.desc: Learn all of Emeril's tricks of the trade: chicken-fried steaks and oysters with creamy gravy, quick and easy broccoli and cheese soup, and homemade gnocchi with marinara sauce

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Join Emeril and young chef CJ Calhoun for a meal any kid would love! Dig into Bacon and Herb Roast Turkey Breast, Oven Roasted Crispy Broccoli, and end with Caramelized Sweet Potatoes.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Join Emeril and Voodoo priestesses Brandi and Maggie as they explore a menu inspired by their annual Voodoo Fest in New Orleans. Get ready to try Black-Eyed Pea Jambalaya and Ham Hock and Cabbage Soup.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Join Emeril and Big Freedia in the kitchen as he whips up her favorite meal of Fried Fish with Smothered Greens and Andouille Potato Salad. Emeril will also share his recipe for Bounce Gumbo, named in Big Freedia's honor.

programme.title: Emeril Cooks
programme.desc: Join Emeril and Abita CEO Dave Blossman as they explore a menu inspired by his beers. Get ready to try Creole Duck Breast with Beer and Cherry Glaze and Bratwurst Cooked in Beer with Apple Sauerkraut.

programme.title: Los Cargadores
programme.desc: Estos trabajadores metidos a cargadores, empezaron desde muy abajo en la escala social, pero un buen dÃ­a se fijaron una meta, aunque tardaron mas tiempo del debido en alcanzarla, ya que algunas veces cuando terminaban con la obligacion, seguian con la "devociÃ³n" de decir "salud" y divertirse a su manera, para olvidar un poco sus penas; al alcanzar la ansiada meta, empiezan a tener otra clase de "amigos" que con el cuento de querer ayudarlos, solo sirven para perjudicarlos

programme.title: El Coyote Mente Diabolica
programme.desc: Bulmaro Reyes comandante retirado de la policía, debido a su violento carácter que quiso asesinar al hombre que mato a su familia, se ve obligado a ir en busca de aquel hombre que se ha fugado y que por sanguinario le llaman el coyote. apoyado por su excompañero en la policía, el comandante Ríos inicia una insólita persecución a caballo en busca del asesino.

programme.title: La Ley de mi Texana
programme.desc: El padre de Juan Manuel fue asesinado años atrás, él vio el crimen y testificó en contra del asesino al que condenaron a prisión. Juan Manuel guarda dentro de su texana una onza de plata que le regaló su padre.

programme.title: Leyenda de un Pistolero
programme.desc: El mejor pistolero de la región, enseña y educa a su único hijo para que siga sus pasos, enfrentándose a todo tipo de asesinos, sin embargo, aparece uno que es igual o mejor que ellos, quien secuestra a la novia de su hijo y la lleva por caminos inimaginables, desencadenándose una historia amor y terror al mismo tiempo.

programme.title: Este Vampiro es un Tiro
programme.desc: Un vampiro vive en una hacienda abandonada y se aburre así que se va en busca de aventuras al pueblo.
